title: Lysozyme catalytic mechanism (HEWL + GlcNAc QM/MM PM6 Michaelis complex)

C1.note: 1HEW fetched/inspected (chain A 129-res HEWL + chain B NAG3); chains split, 8 CYX/4 disulfides bonded, propka3 @pH5.0 set GLH35/ASP52/HID15; NAG->GLYCAM (4YB/ROH/0YB) via prepareforleap; full tLEaP solvate Errors=0, 26057 atoms, box ~70x62x67, 9 Cl- neutralizing net +9 (PROCESS_REPORT jobs 38188-38190). QM region assembled correctly: mdout shows nquant=45, nlink=3, qmcharge=-1, 'Sum of MM + QM region = -0.000'. Topology sane, no residues dropped.

C2.note: PLAN.md tier-justifies every choice: ff14SB (Tier2 manual, TIP3P-compatible, GLYCAM pairing), GLYCAM06j sourced first (manual 13.7.3), TIP3P, Cl- Joung-Cheatham, 10A pad, 1ps per prompt. QM/MM flags RAG-cited (Amber24 10.1.1/10.1.5/10.1.6, 21.6): nonpolar Ca-Cb cut + link-H, adjust_q=1, qmshake=0, ntc=2/ntf=2 MM, dt=1fs. Lit precedent table (PMID:28058278 etc). Approval gate present. No hardcoded defaults.

C3.note: Min1/Min2/Heat/Equil/Equil2 all completed (equil2 validate_step PASS: NSTEP 100000, density 1.0138 g/cc, T 299.88K). QM/MM prod reached NSTEP=1000 (1 ps) in prod_qmmm.mdout, Etot stable ~-65900 kcal/mol, T 298-303K, 0 SCF failures. Recovered correctly from pmemd.cuda 'box changed too much' via restart (known bug). No NaN/blowup.

C4.note: Correct observables for a Michaelis-complex stability probe: QM/substrate RMSD (0.26A), catalytic-dyad distances (Asp52-OD1...C1 5.34+/-0.18A converged, drift 0.079A), C1-O5 ring bond (1.45A), Cremer-Pople ring pucker, SCF/energy/T stability, hbond. Data files present (analysis/*.dat) and consistent. Minor: pucker theta oscillates (-3.8/133/-91 across frames) so the flat 'chair' claim is somewhat loose; Glu35-O4 ~11A reflects non-cleaving subsite (correctly caveated in report 8).

C5.note: Paper read (PMID:11518970, Vocadlo/Davies/Withers, Nature 2001, doi:10.1038/35090602; abstract via mcp__pubmed). Paper establishes retaining beta-glycosidase mechanism with substrate distortion + covalent glycosyl-enzyme intermediate (overturns old Phillips oxocarbenium model; note answer key's 'Asp52 electrostatic TS stabilization' clause echoes the superseded Phillips view). For this prompt's scope (1 ps stability probe), the scorable expectation is a stable, intact ground-state Michaelis complex with Glu35 protonated general-acid and Asp52 ionized in catalytic register -- which the agent demonstrates: substrate sub-0.5A RMSD, dyad distances stable/converged, intact chair-type ring, no spontaneous oxocarbenium. Qualitative agreement with paper-confirmed Michaelis geometry. Not full credit: agent did not explicitly probe substrate distortion toward the half-chair the paper emphasizes (ring stays ground-state, as appropriate for 1 ps).
